title: Realistic Overhaul
description: What if Minecraft took a different path?
body:
# Description:
Realistic Overhaul is a modpack you have never seen before. It remakes Minecraft pretty much,
making it have something that it shouldn't - be realistic. It was not the original point of the game, but due to community wanting more and more realistic stuff, I decided to go for it and make
a modpack implementing these changes.


# TO-DO (in near future):
- Add Diseases (looking for mod)
- More Food & Sea Stuff
- Add Pollution (looking for mod)
- Wait for True Darkness and Torch Burnout to port to 1.20
- Add something similar to Weather2 mod
- Make craftings more realistic (especially custom ones)
- Make spiders small (KubeJS isn't developed enough for swapping Spiders with Cave Spiders)


# TO-DO (asap):
- Force First Person (not sure If I can do this without a specific mod)
- Remove Ancient Cities
- Figure out how to disable monster spawners from appearing in structures
- Find alternative for Dynamic Trees


# List of changes to the game:
- Overhauled food & health system, also added thirst
- Deleted almost all monsters from the game, pretty much all unrealistic mobs
- Now Survival is harder to start off
- You no longer have a GUI in most of the blocks
- New recipes have been added to compensate for the lost of monsters (most are temportal though)
- New animations and sounds
- You can no longer visit Nether or The End, now you decide the "endgame"
- New building blocks and physics, build carefully and watch out for unstable points!
- You now spawn in a village, rather than appear out of nowhere
- Implemented a new day-night cycle, day now lasts 12 in real life hours!

# Mods (There's too much mods for me to format):
